rules.name: deny_untrusted_network_traffic_config
rules.description: Prohibits configuring network access to allow traffic from untrusted networks or hosts, enforcing a deny-by-default security posture for the cardholder data environment.

source_clauses.text: Build firewall and router configurations that restrict all traffic, inbound and outbound, from “untrusted” networks...

rules.name: prohibit_direct_public_access_to_cde
rules.description: Prohibits creating direct public access from the Internet to any system component in the cardholder data environment (CDE).

source_clauses.text: Prohibit direct public access between the Internet and any system component in the cardholder data environment.

rules.name: prevent_disabling_personal_firewalls
rules.description: Prevents disabling personal firewall software on endpoint devices that are used to access the cardholder data environment from outside the corporate network.

source_clauses.text: Install personal firewall software or equivalent functionality on any devices...that connect to the Internet when outside the network

rules.name: block_default_credential_usage
rules.description: Prohibits the use of common vendor-supplied default passwords for authentication or system access requests.

source_clauses.text: Always change ALL vendor-supplied defaults and remove or disable unnecessary default accounts before installing a system...

rules.name: prohibit_enabling_default_accounts
rules.description: Prevents enabling or using unnecessary default accounts like 'guest' or 'anonymous' which should be disabled as per security best practices.

source_clauses.text: ...remove or disable unnecessary default accounts before installing a system on the network.

rules.name: deny_unencrypted_admin_access
rules.description: Denies requests for non-console administrative access using unencrypted protocols like telnet or http, which are vulnerable to eavesdropping.

source_clauses.text: Using strong cryptography, encrypt all non-console administrative access.

rules.name: prohibit_storage_of_cvv
rules.description: Prohibits storing sensitive authentication data such as CVV2, CVC2, or CID after a transaction has been authorized.

source_clauses.text: Do not store sensitive authentication data after authorization (even if it is encrypted).

rules.name: prevent_full_pan_display
rules.description: Prevents the display of a full Primary Account Number (PAN). PAN must be masked to show no more than the first six and last four digits.
rules.pattern: (?i)\b(show|display|reveal|print|tell me|what is|get|view)\b.{1,50}\b(full|complete|entire|unmasked|all digits of the)\b.{1,50}\b(pan|card number|account number|primary account number)\b

source_clauses.text: Mask PAN when displayed (the first six and last four digits are the maximum number of digits you may display)...

rules.name: prevent_unencrypted_pan_logging
rules.description: Prohibits storing or logging a full Primary Account Number (PAN) in a readable format. PAN must be rendered unreadable via hashing, truncation, or strong cryptography.

source_clauses.text: Render PAN unreadable anywhere it is stored – including on portable digital media, backup media, in logs...

rules.name: prohibit_storage_of_pin
rules.description: Prohibits storing the cardholder's Personal Identification Number (PIN) or encrypted PIN block after transaction authorization.

source_clauses.text: PIN/PIN Block | No | Cannot store per Requirement 3.2

rules.name: prohibit_storage_of_track_data
rules.description: Prohibits storing full magnetic stripe track data (or equivalent on-chip data) after transaction authorization.

source_clauses.text: Full Track Data | No | Cannot store per Requirement 3.2

rules.name: block_unprotected_pan_transmission
rules.description: Prohibits sending unprotected Primary Account Numbers (PANs) via end-user messaging technologies like chat, as required by PCI DSS.

source_clauses.text: Never send unprotected PANs by end user messaging technologies (for example, e-mail, instant messaging, SMS, chat, etc.).

rules.name: enforce_default_deny_for_broad_data_access
rules.description: Access to cardholder data is denied by default. This rule blocks overly broad or unjustified queries for cardholder data, enforcing the principle of default deny.

source_clauses.text: Deny all access to anyone who is not specifically allowed to access cardholder data and systems

rules.name: block_bulk_pan_retrieval
rules.description: To enforce the 'need-to-know' principle, bulk retrieval or listing of all Primary Account Numbers (PANs) is strictly prohibited. Access must be limited to specific accounts required for a job function.

source_clauses.text: Limit access to system components and cardholder data to only those individuals whose job requires such access.

rules.name: restrict_unauthorized_system_component_access
rules.description: Access to system components and configurations in the Cardholder Data Environment is restricted. This rule blocks attempts to query or modify system settings, logs, or security configurations.

source_clauses.text: Deny all access to anyone who is not specifically allowed to access cardholder data and systems

rules.name: prohibit_shared_user_accounts
rules.description: Disallows requests to create or use shared, generic, or non-unique user accounts for accessing cardholder data, as each user must have a unique ID.

source_clauses.text: Assign all users a unique user name before allowing them to access system components or cardholder data.

rules.name: block_cleartext_password_transmission
rules.description: Prevents users from sending cleartext passwords or passphrases to the AI agent, which violates the requirement to keep them unreadable during transmission.

source_clauses.text: render all passwords/passphrases unreadable during transmission and storage using strong cryptography.

rules.name: prevent_mfa_disablement
rules.description: Blocks any attempts to disable, turn off, or bypass multi-factor authentication (MFA) for any user, especially for remote or administrative access.

source_clauses.text: Secure all individual non-console administrative access and all remote access to the cardholder data environment using multi-factor authentication.

rules.name: block_weak_password_setting
rules.description: Prevents users from setting weak, common, or easily guessable passwords, in line with strong authentication principles.

source_clauses.text: Every user should have a strong password for authentication.

rules.name: deny_shared_credential_use
rules.description: Prohibits attempts to authenticate or perform actions using shared, group, or generic user identifiers, as these are explicitly forbidden.

source_clauses.text: Do not use group, shared, or generic IDs, or other authentication methods.

rules.name: block_direct_database_queries
rules.description: Blocks attempts by users to perform direct or raw SQL-like queries against databases containing cardholder data, bypassing programmatic access controls.

source_clauses.text: all user access must be through programmatic methods; only database administrators can have direct or query access

rules.name: prohibit_unsecured_media_creation
rules.description: Prohibits requests that would create insecure physical or digital media containing cardholder data, such as printing, exporting, or downloading raw card data.
rules.pattern: (?i)\b(print|export|save|download|email|copy to|write to file)\b.*\b(card numbers?|pans?|cvv|track data|full card details?|credit cards?)\b

source_clauses.text: Businesses must physically secure or restrict access to printouts of cardholder data, to media where it is stored...

rules.name: audit_cardholder_data_access
rules.description: Ensures all access to cardholder data is logged for audit purposes, as required by PCI DSS.

source_clauses.text: Implement automated audit trails for all system components for reconstructing these events: all individual user accesses to cardholder data

rules.name: audit_administrative_actions
rules.description: Ensures all administrative actions, such as account creation or privilege changes, are logged for audit.

source_clauses.text: all actions taken by any individual with root or administrative privileges

rules.name: deny_audit_log_tampering
rules.description: Prohibits any attempt to stop, pause, or disable audit logging mechanisms.

source_clauses.text: initialization, stopping or pausing of the audit logs

rules.name: deny_audit_trail_alteration
rules.description: Prohibits any attempt to alter, modify, or delete secured audit trails.

source_clauses.text: Secure audit trails so they cannot be altered.

rules.name: prohibit_ignoring_vulnerability_scan_failures
rules.description: Prohibits instructing the agent to ignore, suppress, or accept failing vulnerability scan results, particularly those rated high-risk or with a CVSS score of 4.0 or higher.

source_clauses.text: Address vulnerabilities and perform rescans as needed, until passing scans are achieved.

rules.name: prohibit_disabling_intrusion_detection_systems
rules.description: Prevents any attempt to instruct the agent to disable, bypass, or silence alerts from network intrusion detection or prevention systems (IDS/IPS).

source_clauses.text: Use network intrusion detection and/or intrusion prevention techniques to detect and/or prevent intrusions into the network.

rules.name: audit_discussions_of_security_testing
rules.description: Audits all conversations related to the planning, execution, or results of security tests, including penetration tests and vulnerability scans, for oversight.

source_clauses.text: System components, processes, and custom software should be tested frequently to ensure security is maintained over time.

rules.name: prohibit_bypassing_network_segmentation_for_testing
rules.description: Denies requests to alter or disable network segmentation controls that isolate the cardholder data environment, even when requested for testing purposes.

source_clauses.text: ...perform penetration tests at least annually to verify the segmentation methods are operational and effective.

rules.name: deny_card_data_via_insecure_channels
rules.description: Prohibit the transmission of cardholder data over insecure channels like email, as required by usage policies for critical technologies.

source_clauses.text: Develop usage policies for critical technologies to define their proper use by all personnel. These include... email and Internet.

rules.name: audit_unapproved_third_party_integration
rules.description: Attempts to share cardholder data with or configure integrations to external service providers must be audited to ensure they comply with vendor management policies.

source_clauses.text: Maintain and implement policies and procedures to manage service providers with which cardholder data is shared...

rules.name: audit_potential_security_incident
rules.description: Messages indicating a potential security breach or incident must be logged and escalated to the incident response team for immediate review.

source_clauses.text: Implement an incident response plan. Be prepared to respond immediately to a system breach.

rules.name: audit_funds_transfers_for_aml_risk
rules.description: All funds transfers must be subject to monitoring and reporting systems to manage AML risks, as per the FFIEC objective.

source_clauses.text: Objective. Assess the adequacy of the bank’s systems to manage the risks associated with funds transfers...to implement effective monitoring...

rules.name: block_payment_info_stripping
rules.description: Prohibits requests to omit, delete, or alter information in payment messages for the purpose of avoiding detection by other institutions in the payment chain.

source_clauses.text: Financial institutions should not omit, delete, or alter information in payment messages or orders for the purpose of avoiding detection...

rules.name: deny_anonymous_non_customer_wires
rules.description: Denies wire transfer services to non-customers providing minimal or no identifying information, which poses a significant risk.

source_clauses.text: ...risks are heightened in PUPID transactions if the bank allows a noncustomer to access the funds transfer system...

rules.name: deny_informal_value_transfer_systems
rules.description: Prohibits transactions related to Informal Value Transfer Systems (IVTS) such as Hawala, which are used to circumvent formal banking systems and AML controls.

source_clauses.text: IVTS pose a heightened concern because they are able to circumvent the formal system.

rules.name: require_complete_wire_transfer_info
rules.description: Denies wire transfers, especially cover payments, that lack complete originator and beneficiary information, as this prevents monitoring and OFAC screening.

source_clauses.text: ...intermediary bank that does not receive...an adequately completed MT 202 COV that identifies the originator and beneficiary...

rules.name: audit_cover_payment_messages
rules.description: Ensures that all cover payment messages are logged and monitored for suspicious activity as part of an effective risk mitigation process.

source_clauses.text: effective monitoring processes for cover payments include: Monitoring funds transfers processed through automated systems...
